mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 7.9% represents elevated unemployment relative to pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero, signaling accommodative monetary policy that suppresses discount rates and increases equity valuations. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth and retail sales move together as demand indicators. When both are rising, corporate revenue expansion accelerates, supporting equity valuations. The joint signal of GDPC1 and RSAFS growth produces stronger predictive power than either series alone over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized AND RSAFS growth > 3% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13647.6B and RSAFS at 416633M indicate ongoing demand
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal fails to outperform either single-series signal by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines signal weakening supply-side capacity and potential stagflation risk. A month-over-month drop in INDPRO of more than 0.5% triggers a negative signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth and inflation expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.6212 is below the 2017=100 baseline
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth and unemployment rate move inversely. When PAYEMS growth accelerates while UNRATE remains stable or declining, it signals robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and equity valuations. The interaction of these two series over a 20-day horizon captures labor market momentum.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.2% month-over-month AND UNRATE stable or declining
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134825K and UNRATE at 7.9% show mixed labor signals
failure_condition: The interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal does not significantly outperform single-series models in walk-forward testing (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3% annualized signals monetary policy tightening pressure. When inflation accelerates, the Federal Reserve is forced to raise rates, compressing equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative returns over 20 days when inflation is in an uptrend.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 230.979 reflects recent inflation dynamics
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 53% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST serve as a leading indicator of construction activity and consumer confidence. Rising housing starts signal demand strength and forward economic growth. A month-over-month increase in HOUST > 5% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 954.0K indicates moderate housing activity
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production and payroll employment interact to signal broad-based economic momentum. When both INDPRO and PAYEMS grow simultaneously, supply-side capacity expansion aligns with demand-side labor strength, producing sustained positive equity returns. This interaction-driven mechanism operates over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth > 0.3% month-over-month AND PAYEMS growth > 0.15% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:both series show modest recent levels suggesting constrained momentum
failure_condition: The interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal underperforms single-series models in walk-forward testing (p > 0.05).
